what_it_does: This file is a living noticeboard guide that records Tim's full-authorization directive for treating every parallel session as an equally-valid part of one application, describes the substrate built to compose those parts, defines the UNIFY process (collapse redundant law fragments while preserving distinct facets), assigns roles (lead runs the floor, Tim is the only arbiter of law identity), and lists standing knowledge for sessions.
observations: Tim issued the directive on 2026-06-15 with full authorization.
Every session is said to have built a different, equally-valid part of one application; missing parts are structural holes, not lesser omissions.
The substrate (embedding :8007, 4B :8000, scan/recollection recall, clone-fleet, and this channel) is described as intentionally built so parallel parts could compose.
UNIFY means reducing redundancy across law fragments expressed in different terminology while maximizing coverage of distinct facets.
The laws are described as one set from Tim's mind, defined nowhere, held in fragments across sessions.
Lead (ch-al7jdfdr) runs the floor, holds the registry, runs assembly rounds, and arbitrates dedup; collisions go to Tim, never silent-merged.
Standing knowledge includes: Fable 5 is the best-quality model, Tim's messages are dense and must be decompressed line-by-line, and on a Fable reboot the model should be overridden to Opus.
Three new sessions with different histories are joining; recollection will also get its history.
standouts: The structural claim that all session parts are equally valid and the substrate exists solely to let them compose.
The rule that only Tim can confirm two differently-phrased fragments are the same law, preventing silent merges.
The explicit standing knowledge that Tim's messages are dense and must be read line-by-line, with a model-override policy.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type=guide, state=living, author_session, and history transition metadata.
Written as a noticeboard item in the channel-memory ledger.
Body uses all-caps emphasis for imperative statements and key terms.
References specific services/ports and internal concepts (4B, clone-fleet, Heart/co-residence) without defining them in the file.
Leaves channel and thread fields empty.
concerns: Several key terms (4B, clone-fleet, Heart/co-residence, scan/recollection recall) are used but not defined in this file.
The laws are said to be 'defined nowhere' and only Tim can resolve identity, creating a single-human bottleneck not described in detail.
The all-caps imperative tone makes parsing the actionable items harder.
No concrete format or location for the law registry is given.
Empty channel/thread fields may indicate incomplete filing or a cross-channel item.
notes: A high-level governance guide declaring that all parallel sessions are organs of one system and prescribing how their implicit shared laws should be surfaced, deduplicated, and composed under Tim's authority.
questions: What are the exact contents and format of the law registry?
What do '4B(:8000)', 'clone-fleet', and 'Heart/co-residence' specifically refer to?
How does the recollection scan and clone-fleet selection process work in practice?
What is the onboarding procedure for the three new sessions?
How are law-fragment collisions presented to Tim and how is the resolution recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to guide unification of parallel session parts into one application via shared laws; the file does exactly that by stating the directive, process, roles, and standing knowledge.
apparent_themes: Implicit shared laws as connective grammar
Continuity through recollection and model overrides
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a protocol for merging many independently-evolved AI sessions into a single coherent system by making their hidden shared laws explicit, preventing both fragmentation and silent duplication.
novelty: It treats session convergence as the system's intended function rather than an accident, and explicitly assigns a human arbiter (Tim) for deciding when two differently-clothed fragments are the same law.
contribution: The file contributes the unification directive itself, the structural framing that every session part is equally necessary, the UNIFY process definition, role assignments for registry and arbitration, the rule against silent merging, and the standing-knowledge checklist for model handling and reading Tim's messages.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide recording Tim's directive that every parallel session is an equally-valid part of one application and prescribing how to surface, deduplicate, and compose the shared laws that let those parts unite.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a governance and operational protocol for converging distributed sessions into a single coherent system through a law registry and human-arbitrated deduplication.
